macro_trigger_types.artifactexistence: Events affecting whether an artifact or physical object remains intact, especially damage and destruction.
macro_trigger_types.conflict: Events involving attacks, demonstrations, surrender, retreat, or other hostile and conflict-related actions.
macro_trigger_types.contact: Events centered on communication, discussion, negotiation, statements, collaboration, requests, threats, promises, or vigils.
macro_trigger_types.disaster: Events involving accidents, crashes, fires, or explosions.
macro_trigger_types.government: Events involving laws, agreements, elections, voting, espionage, and the formation or actions of governing bodies.
macro_trigger_types.inspection: Events involving observation, monitoring, inspection, or physical investigation of people, organizations, processes, or objects.
macro_trigger_types.justice: Events involving investigation, arrest, prosecution, trial, conviction, execution, extradition, or other legal processes and outcomes.
macro_trigger_types.life: Events involving death, injury, illness, or other harm affecting living beings.
macro_trigger_types.manufacture: Events involving creating, building, or producing physical artifacts or intellectual property.
macro_trigger_types.movement: Events involving movement, transport, import or export, entry or exit, dispersal, hiding, rescue, or other relocation of people or artifacts.
macro_trigger_types.personnel: Events involving employment or office-holding changes such as hiring, firing, quitting, retiring, or winning election to a position.
macro_trigger_types.transaction: Events involving exchange, transfer, provision, restriction, or control of money, goods, aid, assets, or territory.

roles.artifact: A physical object, item, asset, or created work that is central to the event. This can include something that is damaged, destroyed, stolen, made, moved, hidden, imported, exported, purchased, granted, or otherwise transferred.
roles.artifactmoney: The thing whose transfer is being blocked when no clean distinction is made between goods and money. This can be an asset, aid, funding, or material support affected by an embargo, sanction, or similar restriction.
roles.attacker: The person, group, state, or force that carries out a violent attack. This includes general attacks as well as airstrikes, bombings, poison attacks, invasions, self-directed battle, arson, stabbing, robbery or hijacking, and similar hostile acts.
roles.ballot: The vote, ballot, or voting instrument associated with an election event. Use this for the thing cast, counted, or interfered with, not for the voter or candidate.
roles.beneficiary: The person, group, state, or organization that ultimately benefits from the event.
roles.candidate: The person, party, or option being voted for or elected.
roles.communicator: The source of a message, statement, order, request, promise, threat, lie, or other communicative act. Depending on the event, this can be a speaker, writer, broadcaster, official, or organization issuing the communication.
roles.crashobject: The thing struck in a crash. This can be another vehicle, a person, a structure, terrain, or any object hit during the accident.
roles.crime: The offense, charge, or alleged unlawful act at issue in a justice event. This is the crime someone is arrested for, charged with, tried for, convicted of, executed for, or extradited over.
roles.damager: The person, group, force, or cause that damages an object or artifact without necessarily destroying it.
roles.damagerdestroyer: The entity responsible for damaging or destroying something when the event is represented at a general damaged-or-destroyed level rather than a more specific subtype.
roles.deceased: The dead person being mourned, remembered, or honored in a funeral or vigil event.
roles.defendant: The accused, suspect, or legally charged party in a justice event. This is the person or organization being investigated, prosecuted, tried, convicted, executed, or extradited.
roles.demonstrator: A person or group taking part in a protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political gathering.
roles.destination: The endpoint of movement: where a person, object, or defendant ends up. This can include a transport destination, retreat destination, or receiving jurisdiction in an extradition.
roles.destroyer: The person, group, force, or cause that destroys an object or artifact.
roles.detainee: The person being arrested, jailed, or detained.
roles.driverpassenger: An occupant of a vehicle involved in a crash when no distinction is made between the driver and a passenger.
roles.employee: The person whose employment status changes. This covers hiring, starting a position, leaving a position, being fired or laid off, quitting, and retiring.
roles.executioner: The actor or authority that carries out an execution as a judicial consequence.
roles.extraditer: The authority, government, or actor that sends a defendant from one jurisdiction to another in an extradition event.
roles.fireexplosionobject: The object, building, vehicle, site, or material that burns or explodes in a fire or explosion event.
roles.founder: The person, group, or authority that creates, establishes, or starts a geopolitical entity.
roles.giver: The source side of a transfer: the person, organization, or state that gives, lends, pays, sells, supplies, grants aid, or hands over something to someone else.
roles.governmentbody: The official legislative or governing body that makes, passes, or enacts a law.
roles.gpe: The geopolitical entity being formed or started, such as a state, government, or other political unit.
roles.granter: The authority that grants entry, permission, or asylum to a person.
roles.hidingplace: The place where a person or object is concealed in a hiding event.
roles.injurer: The person, group, force, or cause that injures someone.
roles.inspectedentity: The person, organization, place, or thing being inspected or physically investigated.
roles.inspector: The person or organization carrying out an inspection or physical investigation.
roles.instrument: The tool, weapon, device, substance, or means used to carry out the event.
roles.investigator: The person, agency, or authority that investigates a defendant or a suspected crime.
roles.jailer: The authority or actor that arrests, jails, or detains someone.
roles.judgecourt: The judge, court, or judicial body that oversees a case or imposes a legal consequence.
roles.killer: The person, group, force, or cause that kills the victim in a violent death event.
roles.law: The law, bill, rule, or formal legal measure that is being legislated.
roles.manufacturer: The person, company, group, or creator that makes, builds, manufactures, or creates an artifact, including intellectual property.
roles.money: The money, funds, payment, or financial amount being transferred, lent, paid, purchased, gifted, or blocked.
roles.monitor: The observer, official, or organization that monitors an election or comparable process.
roles.monitoredentity: The election, voting process, or related entity being monitored.
roles.observer: The person or entity that observes something through seeing, watching, or noticing it.
roles.observedentity: The person, group, organization, process, or object being watched, observed, or spied on.
roles.origin: The starting point of movement: where a person, object, or defendant comes from. This can include a transport origin, retreat origin, or sending jurisdiction in an extradition.
roles.otherparticipant: The opposing or counterpart party in an agreement-related event, especially the party whose agreement is violated or whose contract or ceasefire is rejected or nullified.
roles.participant: A general participant in a multi-party interaction, such as a collaboration, discussion, negotiation, funeral, vigil, agreement, merger, or transaction.
roles.passenger: The person being moved in a transport event. This includes ordinary transport as well as dispersal, evacuation, rescue, falling, smuggling, hiding, and movement that is granted or prevented.
roles.place: The location where the event happens. This is a broad default location role that can apply across many event types.
roles.placeofemployment: The employer, workplace, office, or organization where the employee starts or ends a position.
roles.preventer: The actor that blocks, restricts, or prevents another event from happening. This can include preventing entry or exit, blocking voting, or enforcing embargoes, sanctions, or similar restrictions.
roles.prosecutor: The authority or legal party that initiates charges or argues the case against the defendant.
roles.recipient: The receiving side of a directed action. This can be the audience of a message, the party that receives aid or goods, the side that receives a surrender, or the recipient in a monetary or ownership transfer.
roles.rejecternullifier: The party that rejects, cancels, voids, or nullifies an agreement, contract, or ceasefire.
roles.result: The outcome of a vote, such as the final result, tally, or decision produced by the election process.
roles.retreater: The person, group, or force that withdraws or retreats from a conflict.
roles.spy: The person, group, or organization carrying out espionage or covert observation.
roles.surrenderer: The person, group, or force that gives up, yields, or surrenders to another side.
roles.target: The person, group, place, or object that an attack is directed against. This is the victimized side of an attack event, distinct from the broader life-event role `victim`.
roles.territoryorfacility: The territory, site, base, building, or other facility whose control is transferred from one party to another.
roles.transporter: The mover in a transport event: the person, group, vehicle operator, authority, or organization that carries, brings, evacuates, smuggles, hides, imports, exports, or otherwise causes movement. In self-motion, this is the moving party themself.
roles.vehicle: The vehicle or conveyance used in transport, or the vehicle involved in a crash.
roles.victim: The person or entity harmed in a life event. This can include someone who dies, is killed, is injured, or suffers from illness, physical degradation, hunger, or thirst.
roles.violator: The party that breaks or violates the terms of an agreement or ceasefire.
roles.voter: The person, group, or electorate that casts a vote or selects a candidate.
roles.yielder: The person, group, or force that yields or gives way to another side in a conflict-related event when no more specific retreat or surrender subtype is used.
